welcomeMessage.en: Today's class is all about the rhythm and vibes!
welcomeMessage.zh: 今天的课程完全是关于节奏和氛围的！

welcomeMessage.en: We're going to talk about music, live concerts, and how songs make us feel deeply nostalgic.
welcomeMessage.zh: 我们将谈论音乐、现场音乐会以及歌曲是如何让我们产生深深的怀旧感的。

welcomeMessage.en: Get ready to share the soundtrack of your life!
welcomeMessage.zh: 准备好分享你生活的主题曲吧！

questionsToThinkAbout.en: Can you name a song that instantly reminds you of your childhood?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 你能说出一首立刻让你想起童年的歌曲吗？

questionsToThinkAbout.en: Do you prefer going to a crowded, energetic concert or listening to music quietly at home?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 你更喜欢去拥挤、充满活力的演唱会，还是在家安静地听音乐？

questionsToThinkAbout.en: What kind of music helps you focus when you are working or studying?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 在工作或学习时，什么样的音乐能帮你集中注意力？

questionsToThinkAbout.en: Tell us about a time when you thought an experience would be bad, but it turns out it was actually great!
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 告诉我们一个你以为会很糟糕，但结果却很棒的经历！

questionsToThinkAbout.en: What is a trendy song right now that has lyrics you really enjoy (or lyrics you find difficult to understand)?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 现在流行的一首歌中，哪首歌的歌词你非常喜欢（或者觉得很难懂）？

vocabulary.example: The melody is beautiful, but the lyrics of this song are very sad.

vocabulary.definition: Needing much effort or skill to accomplish, deal with, or understand.
vocabulary.example: Rap songs are very difficult to sing because the words are so fast.

vocabulary.definition: Feeling, evoking, or characterized by a sentimental longing for the past.
vocabulary.translation: 怀旧的 / 引起怀旧之情的
vocabulary.example: Listening to 90s pop music makes me feel so nostalgic.

vocabulary.definition: Very fashionable or up to date.
vocabulary.example: K-pop and hip-hop are very trendy right now among young people.

vocabulary.definition: Showing or involving great activity or vitality.
vocabulary.translation: 精力充沛的 / 充满活力的
vocabulary.example: I like to listen to energetic music when I am working out at the gym.
I will go to her concert, no matter how much the ticket costs.

vocabulary.definition: A musical performance given in public.
vocabulary.example: We bought front-row tickets for the rock concert this weekend.

vocabulary.definition: Full of people, leaving little or no room for movement.
vocabulary.example: The subway was so crowded after the concert that we couldn't even move.
I thought I wouldn't like jazz music, but it turns out I love it!

vocabulary.definition: A person's emotional state or the atmosphere of a place as communicated to and felt by others.
vocabulary.translation: 氛围 / 气氛 / 感觉
vocabulary.example: This coffee shop has a very relaxing and chill vibe.

vocabulary.definition: Adapt to the prevailing level of light and become able to see clearly. Pay particular attention to.
vocabulary.example: Listening to soft piano music helps me focus on my homework.
Whenever I hear this song, I instantly feel happy.

vocabulary.definition: Cause someone to think of (someone or something).
vocabulary.example: This classic song reminds me of my high school days.

vocabulary.definition: The state of being a child.
vocabulary.example: I spent my childhood playing in the park with my neighborhood friends.

vocabulary.translation: 欢快的 / 节奏轻快的
vocabulary.example: I need some upbeat music to wake me up in the morning.

sessionSummary: Today's class was all about the rhythm and vibes! We discussed our favorite types of music, going to live events, and how different songs can instantly change our moods.
We talked about the excitement of going to a live concert. Even though stadiums can get incredibly crowded, everyone agreed that the energetic vibe makes it a fantastic and unforgettable experience.

We also explored how hearing an old song can remind us of our childhood, making us feel deeply nostalgic. It turns out that some of the trendy songs we used to listen to years ago actually have very difficult lyrics to memorize!
Finally, Kiki, Yating, and Peter shared how upbeat music helps them focus while working or studying, no matter what difficult task they are trying to complete.

keyVocabulary.example: "The melody is beautiful, but the lyrics of this song are very sad."
"Rap songs are very difficult to sing because the words are so fast."

keyVocabulary.translation: 怀旧的 / 引起怀旧之情的
keyVocabulary.example: "Listening to 90s pop music makes me feel so nostalgic."
"K-pop and hip-hop are very trendy right now among young people."

keyVocabulary.translation: 精力充沛的 / 充满活力的
keyVocabulary.example: "I like to listen to energetic music when I am working out at the gym."
"I will go to her concert, no matter how much the ticket costs."
"We bought front-row tickets for the rock concert this weekend."
"The subway was so crowded after the concert that we couldn't even move."
"I thought I wouldn't like jazz music, but it turns out I love it!"

keyVocabulary.translation: 氛围 / 气氛 / 感觉
keyVocabulary.example: "This coffee shop has a very relaxing and chill vibe."
"Listening to soft piano music helps me focus on my homework."
"Whenever I hear this song, I instantly feel happy."
"This classic song reminds me of my high school days."
"I spent my childhood playing in the park with my neighborhood friends."

keyVocabulary.example: "I need some upbeat music to wake me up in the morning."

grammarTips.title: Grammar Correction: 'No matter' vs. 'Regardless of'
grammarTips.description: It is a common mistake to say 'no matter of'. In English, you should use 'no matter' followed by a question word (what, how, who, where), OR you can say 'regardless of'. Never mix them together!
grammarTips.incorrect: "I will listen to music no matter of the time."
grammarTips.correct: "I will listen to music NO MATTER WHAT time it is. OR I will listen to music REGARDLESS OF the time."

grammarTips.title: Using 'Remind of'
grammarTips.description: When a song or smell makes you think of a memory, we use the verb 'remind' followed by the object and the preposition 'of'. Don't forget the 'of'!
grammarTips.correct: "This song reminds me OF my childhood."

grammarTips.title: Native Slang: Vibe
grammarTips.description: The word 'vibe' is short for vibration, but native speakers use it constantly as slang to describe the emotional atmosphere of a place, a song, or even a person.
grammarTips.correct: "The restaurant has a really good vibe."

grammarTips.title: Using 'Turns out'
grammarTips.description: This is a fantastic conversational phrase! Use it when the final result of a situation was different from what you originally expected.
grammarTips.incorrect: "I thought it would rain, but the end it was sunny."
grammarTips.correct: "I thought it would rain, but it TURNS OUT it was sunny."

goals: Ensure students stop using 'no matter of' and use 'regardless of' or 'no matter [question word]' correctly.
Help students correctly use 'vibe' to describe emotional atmospheres.
Make sure students don't forget the particle 'of' with 'remind'.

phases.title: Phase 1: Warm-up and Vocabulary (15 Minutes)
phases.description: Introduce the idea of 'vibes' and finding a song's mood.
questions: What kind of music helps you focus? Is it upbeat or relaxing?

phases.title: Phase 2: Nostalgia and Memory (25 Minutes)
phases.description: Practice the 'reminds me of' and 'turns out' phrasing by telling childhood stories related to songs.
questions: Can you name a song that instantly reminds you of your childhood?
Tell us about a time when you thought an experience would be bad, but it turns out it was actually great!

phases.title: Phase 3: Live Events (15 Minutes)
phases.description: Discuss the pros and cons of live concerts. Focus on 'crowded' and 'energetic'.
questions: Do you prefer going to a crowded, energetic concert or listening to music quietly at home?
